name: Ensure RDS Instances accept traffic only from the Application Servers
body:
### Security intent

This control reduces unnecessary exposure by ensuring that **rDS Instances accept traffic only from the Application Servers**.

### Network condition reviewed

The assessment reviews rDS Instances accept traffic only from the Application Servers in AWS. It focuses on rules, endpoints, listeners, or policies that make the service reachable in a riskier way than intended.

### Expected posture

Public reachability should be deliberate, documented, and tightly scoped. Administrative and data-plane paths should use private connectivity, strong TLS, explicit source ranges, and provider-native controls rather than broad internet access.
description: RDS Instances accept traffic only from the Application Servers
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

Allow RDS access only from application security groups.

### Console
1. Open RDS instance security groups.
2. Remove broad CIDR ingress for DB ports.
3. Add inbound rules from application security group IDs only.

### AWS CLI
~~~bash
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id <db-sg-id> --protocol tcp --port <db-port> --source-group <app-sg-id>
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <db-sg-id> --protocol tcp --port <db-port> --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Overview.RDSSecurityGroups.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/
